title: OCI runtime error
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
I can open this specific container in docker and podman under root. However once I try to load it under a working rootless podman configuration it fails to launch with a `Error: OCI runtime error: crun: open executable: Operation not supported`

I can get a container like archlinux to launch in rootless podman no problem so I have some level of a working configuration
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. get a working rootless podman configuration setup

2. run this command `podman run -it docker.io/sandreas/m4b-tool:latest`


**Describe the results you received:**
I ended up getting a `Error: OCI runtime error: crun: open executable: Operation not supported` line printed out after executing that line

**Describe the results you expected:**
I expected it to run the container and print out something like this
```
m4b-tool v.0.4.2



Usage:

  command [options] [arguments]



Options:

  -h, --help            Display this help message

  -q, --quiet           Do not output any message

  -V, --version         Display this application version

      --ansi            Force ANSI output

      --no-ansi         Disable ANSI output

  -n, --no-interaction  Do not ask any interactive question

  -v|vv|vvv, --verbose  Increase the verbosity of messages: 1 for normal output, 2 for more verbose output and 3 for debug



Available commands:

  chapters  Adds chapters to m4b file

  help      Displays help for a command

  list      Lists commands

  merge     Merges a set of files to one single file

  split     Splits an m4b file into parts

```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine

Version:      4.1.1

API Version:  4.1.1

Go Version:   go1.18.3

Git Commit:   f73d8f8875c2be7cd2049094c29aff90b1150241

Built:        Fri Jun 24 02:33:56 2022

OS/Arch:      linux/amd64


```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

    uidmap:

      host_id: 165535

      size: 65536

  kernel: 5.15.53-2-lts

  linkmode: dynamic

  ociRuntime:

    name: crun

    package: /usr/bin/crun is owned by crun 1.4.5-1

      crun version 1.4.5

      commit: c381048530aa750495cf502ddb7181f2ded5b400

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
pacman -Q podman

podman 4.1.1-2
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes but its possible I missed something in the troubleshooting guide, however I should be running the latest version.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
physical arch linux box running a ryzen 7 2700x with 128gb of ram.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @popcorn9499!

Does it work running as root and/or with --privileged?

I cannot reproduce on Fedora 36.
Can you try with runc instead of crun?

> Thanks for reaching out, @popcorn9499!
> 
> Does it work running as root and/or with --privileged?
> 
> I cannot reproduce on Fedora 36.

It works on root with or without --privileged.

However it did not work when in rootless with --privileged either

> Can you try with runc instead of crun?

I am not 100% sure how I would go about doing that I will do more looking and get back to you on that

Edit:
Ended up figuring that out and it doesnt solve my problem entirely but it pushes the error to something slightly different. 
```
exec /usr/local/bin/m4b-tool: operation not supported
```

figured throwing up these logs may be helpful.

podman --log-level=debug run docker.io/sandreas/m4b-tool:latest
https://pastebin.com/LaQeyZxh

podman --log-level=debug --runtime runc run docker.io/sandreas/m4b-tool:latest
https://pastebin.com/Fu0LRpNy

what is the underlying file system?

Could you share the content of `/proc/self/mountinfo`?

and please also include the output for:

```
podman unshare strace -f -s 1000 -v -Z podman run -it docker.io/sandreas/m4b-tool:latest
```

> what is the underlying file system?
> 
> Could you share the content of `/proc/self/mountinfo`?

The underlying filesystem should be zfs as thats the filesystem for the home directory

the contents of /proc/self/mountinfo are https://pastebin.com/XZFRyCQc

> and please also include the output for:
> 
> ```
> podman unshare strace -f -s 1000 -v -Z podman run -it docker.io/sandreas/m4b-tool:latest
> ```

pastebin didnt allow me to upload that large of a file so I had to upload it here. Anyways here ya go https://photos.popicraft.net/u/files/12.26.20-14.07.22-kSEqzrEt9U.txt

the issue seems to be with the underlying file system that doesn't support extended attributes:

```
                                                                               [pid 1018119] lgetxattr("/home/dad/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/4ec04656270d59525a46092484d3c37b49477bc2a20ed8fb0e7828279b3da1c7/diff/usr/local/bin/m4b-tool", "system.posix_acl_access", 0x55cc94dd6560, 4096) = -1 EOPNOTSUPP (Operation not supported)

```

you may try to add `noacl` to the `mountopt` in your `storage.conf` file so fuse-overlayfs won't try to use it.  Altough without extended attributes other things might break.
